paper_title: Implications of Analytical Method Choice and Impediments to Proxy Interpretation

methods.description: This method involves the use of a Niton XL3t GOLDD X-Ray Fluorescence analyzer to perform geochemical analysis on subsampled sediment records from Lake Ighiel. It is used to rapidly assess the geochemical composition of sediments.

steps.description: The Lake Ighiel sediment record was subsampled at 10-cm resolution (55 samples in total) for further sample-based geochemical analysis using a Niton XL3t GOLDD X-Ray Fluorescence analyzer (FPXRF) mounted in a shield and performed at the University of Salford, UK.
steps.searchable_summary: Subsample sediment record at 10-cm resolution for FPXRF analysis

steps.description: After measuring the wet weight, each 1 cm3 sample was oven-dried at ~40°C, ground to fine powder to homogenize the sample and carefully pressed into plastic cups above a 6-µm-thick polyester film, forming a measurement window.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry, grind, and prepare sediment samples for FPXRF analysis
outputs: Prepared sediment samples in plastic cups

steps.description: Each plastic cup was placed in the FPXRF laboratory stand and analyzed under main and low filters, each for 240 s.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze prepared samples using FPXRF under main and low filters

steps.description: The certified reference material (CRM) used was NCS DC73308 (Chinese stream sediment) as it has comparable elemental concentrations and a similar matrix to our lake sediment core.
steps.searchable_summary: Validate FPXRF results using certified reference material

steps.description: Results were acquired including the two-sigma error for each reading, which represents two standard deviations from the mean.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate two-sigma error for FPXRF readings

methods.description: This method involves statistical treatment of the dataset to assess the behavior of each element in relation to the method applied, evaluate performance, and investigate correlations and similarities-dissimilarities between datasets.

steps.description: To assess the behavior of each element in relation to the method applied, the dataset was statistically treated.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform statistical treatment on dataset to assess element behavior

steps.description: To evaluate the performance of each method and to investigate the correlation and similarities-dissimilarities between datasets, Pearson correlation coefficient (r) was applied.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply Pearson correlation coefficient to evaluate method performance

steps.description: Descriptive statistics were used to evaluate data distribution (a normal distribution is defined when skewness equals/is close to 0 and kurtosis is close to 3), assess the overall changes with similarities and differences of the dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: Use descriptive statistics to evaluate data distribution

steps.description: Box plots were used to interrogate the differences and similarities between geochemical outputs and evaluate, how element recovery depends on and/or behaves in terms of the method used, with a close look at the overall data variability and values (median, extreme values, outliers).
steps.searchable_summary: Create box plots to visualize differences and similarities in geochemical outputs

steps.description: To avoid the comparison of depth-based variables in absolute values and possible issues that might arise when comparing different units, the Z-score standardization was used to bring the data to the same unit.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply Z-score standardization to standardize data units
